expected_findings.summary: 2 1099-B lot(s) missing Code W on Form 8949 (disallowed loss $3,030)
expected_findings.detail: The broker reported a wash sale loss disallowed amount on these lots, but Form 8949 column (f) does not carry Code W and column (g) does not add the disallowance back. IRC §1091 requires the disallowed loss to be tracked and added to the replacement-lot basis. This is a mechanical compliance error that routinely triggers IRS CP2000 notices because the IRS wash-sale computer match sees the broker-reported amount and expects it on the return.
Form 8889 requires line 2 (contributions), line 6 (allowable), and line 13 (deduction) to reconcile with the TY2025 statutory limit. line 6 is $8,300 but TY2025 family limit is $8,550.

expected_findings.summary: 1 RSU vest lot(s) with basis near $0 — likely double-counted
expected_findings.detail: The W-2 has a Box 12 code V (RSU / NQSO income) and the 1099-B has same-day sale-to-cover lots with a cost basis near zero. RSU vest income is already included in W-2 Box 1 wages; the 1099-B lot cost basis should equal the Box 1 inclusion amount, not zero. Leaving the basis at zero double-counts the vest as both wage income and a capital gain. This is the single most common mechanically detectable error in the public TurboTax record — a $3,200 refund delta is in Intuit's own public record.

expected_findings.summary: Passive K-1 loss from Synthetic Partners 25 LP must be suspended
expected_findings.detail: The K-1 is marked passive and has a loss. Per IRC §469 passive losses can only offset passive income. If there is no passive income to absorb the loss this year, it must be suspended on Form 8582 and carried forward.

expected_findings.summary: PTET election can effectively uncap the SALT deduction on K-1 income
expected_findings.detail: IRS Notice 2020-75 blessed state-level PTET regimes as a way to pay state tax at the entity level, converting a capped SALT deduction into a federal ordinary business expense that passes through as lower K-1 income. Consider the PTET election with each state where the K-1 generates income.

A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Parker has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Parker is 37 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Casey has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Casey is 37 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.
Parker has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
Casey has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Pass-through income allocated across 2+ PTET states
expected_findings.detail: Multiple states allow a PTET election but apportion the deduction differently. Consult the partnership's state apportionment schedule before electing — the federal benefit stacks per state.
1 qualifying child/ren under age 17. AGI is below the phaseout start, so the full $2,000 per child is available.

expected_findings.summary: LLC available for qualified education expenses
expected_findings.detail: AGI is below the LLC phaseout. Up to $2,000 per return (20% of first $10,000 of qualified education expenses). Non-refundable.

expected_findings.summary: Dependent care credit available (20% at high AGI)
expected_findings.detail: Up to $3,000 of qualified dependent-care expenses can support the credit, though at AGI > $43K the rate is 20%. Requires both spouses to have earned income in MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Need $25,904 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.
